https://github.com/Eugeny/tabby.reason: Electron GUI app; no meaningful headless run

https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty.reason: GUI OpenGL terminal; no meaningful headless run

https://github.com/chubin/cheat.sh.reason: server + data-fetch heavy; only one trivial offline unit test, no honest offline smoke

https://github.com/coder/code-server.reason: vendors all of VS Code (multi-GB, long build); out of scope

https://github.com/genymobile/scrcpy.reason: scrcpy is split into a Java server APK (built with Gradle + Android SDK, deployed via adb) and a C client (meson + SDL2 + FFmpeg). The Android SDK is not in any cached toolchain, and the client requires SDL2 (display) and FFmpeg/libav dev packages not in c_rootfs. At runtime the app opens a GUI window showing the device screen — there is no headless or CLI mode and no meaningful smoke test without a connected Android device.

https://github.com/gyulyvgc/sniffnet.reason: sniffnet is a GUI desktop application built on iced/wgpu. Compiling it on Linux requires system libraries not present in rust_rootfs: libpcap-dev (for the pcap packet-capture crate), libgtk-3-dev (for the rfd native file-dialog crate), and X11/Wayland dev headers (for winit/iced). Even if compiled, the binary opens a graphical window for all normal operation with no meaningful headless mode. This places it in the same deferral category as alacritty, pake, and tabby.

https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-.reason: needs a browser toolchain (Chrome/chromium ~646M); deferred until we invest in a cached playwright_rootfs

https://github.com/mifi/lossless-cut.reason: lossless-cut is an Electron + React GUI video editing application. It uses electron-vite with a renderer targeting chrome148, requires electron-builder install-app-deps (downloads native Electron binaries), and has no headless or CLI mode — the packaged app opens a desktop window for all operations. This is the same category as tabby (Electron, deferred) and pake (Tauri, deferred): a GUI-only desktop app with no meaningful headless execution path.

https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF.reason: needs an OCR toolchain (tesseract/ghostscript/unpaper/qpdf) + large real-OCR suite

https://github.com/ollama/ollama.reason: Ollama requires a two-phase build: cmake for the llama-server C++ backend and CGo compilation for x/imagegen and x/mlxrunner (both have #cgo directives with generated C headers produced by go generate). No cached toolchain covers both Go and cmake/g++. Building with CGO_ENABLED=0 fails because runner-> x/imagegen/mlx and x/mlxrunner/mlx are unconditionally imported with CGo. A new go_c_rootfs toolchain combining Go + cmake + build-essential is required; that decision is out of scope for this run.

https://github.com/protocolbuffers/proto.reason: could not land: claude rc=1, no changes

https://github.com/siddharthvaddem/opens.reason: openscreen is a GUI desktop application for screen recording and demo creation, the TypeScript equivalent of Screen Studio. Its repo contains CMakeLists.txt (cross-platform native screen-capture code), Package.swift (macOS AVFoundation/ScreenCaptureKit Swift wrapper), and package.json (Electron frontend) — the standard shape of an Electron app with native addons. The application opens a graphical window for all normal operation; there is no CLI or headless mode. Same deferral category as lossless-cut, tabby, pake, and alacritty.

https://github.com/tw93/pake.reason: Pake is a Tauri v2 desktop GUI application. Its src-tauri/Cargo.toml depends on tauri 2.10.2 and tauri-build 2.5.5. On Linux, Tauri v2 requires libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev, libgtk-3-dev, and related GTK/display-server headers for compilation — none of which are present in the rust_rootfs toolchain. The resulting app creates an OS window to render the wrapped URL; there is no meaningful headless execution path. This is the same deferral category as alacritty (OpenGL terminal) and tabby (Electron).
